subject: Biology
title: How Big Is a Cell?
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How Big Is a Cell?.

hook.text: Most human cells are about 10 micrometers wide. A hundred could line up on one millimeter.

buildup.text: Bacteria are ten times smaller. Viruses are smaller still—invisible to light microscopes.

discovery.text: The largest single cell? An ostrich egg, about 15 centimeters across.

twist.text: Some nerve cells stretch over a meter long, running from your spine to your toes.

climax.text: Size shapes function. Small cells exchange materials fast; large cells store more.

punchline.text: Life works at scales you can barely imagine.

quiz.question: What is the largest single cell?
